The bearing is positive (+26), which typically means the goal is to the right of my current heading. I need to calibrate the bearing sign convention by making a test turn. Since the bearing is positive, I should try turning right to see if the bearing value decreases (moves closer to zero). If turning right decreases the bearing, then positive bearing means "turn right to align".

I will execute a TURN_RIGHT to test the bearing convention and start aligning with the goal.
